solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1431E - Chess Match
There are two possible solutions to this problem: one in $$$O(n^2)$$$, another one in $$$O(n \log n \log A)$$$. Both solutions are based on the fact that one of the cyclic shifts of the array $$$[1, 2, 3, \dots, n]$$$ is the optimal answer.
Beginning of the proof
Suppose there are $$$k$$$ players from the first team that are weaker than their opponents. Then it's always optimal to choose $$$k$$$ first players as the ones that are weaker than their opponents, and $$$n - k$$$ last players as the ones that are not weaker than their opponents (if that's not the case, we can swap two players that violate this constraint, and the answer won't become worse).
The same applies to the second team: $$$k$$$ last players will be stronger than their opponents, and $$$n - k$$$ first players will not be stronger (the proof is similar here).
Okay, now we have to pair $$$k$$$ first players from the first team with $$$k$$$ last players from the second team, assuming that the players from the first team lose. It's easy to see that if we want to maximize the minimum of $$$b_{p_i} - a_i$$$, then we need to set $$$p_1 = n - k + 1$$$, $$$p_2 = n - k + 2$$$, ..., $$$p_k = n$$$ (if this order is violated, we can swap two adjacent values in $$$p$$$, and the answer won't become worse). The same applies to $$$n - k$$$ last players from the first team and $$$n - k$$$ first players from the second team.
End of the proof
Now, the easy solution is just to try all cyclic shifts as the answer. It was enough to get AC, but it can be improved to $$$O(n \log n \log A)$$$ as follows with binary search on the answer. To check that we can make the answer at least $$$m$$$, we can use the fact that when we increase $$$k$$$, the value of $$$\min\limits_{i = 1}^{k} b_{n - k + i} - a_i$$$ decreases, and the value of $$$\min\limits_{i = 1}^{n - k} a_{k + i} - b_i$$$ increases. We can find the last $$$k$$$ for which $$$\min\limits_{i = 1}^{k} b_{n - k + i} - a_i \ge m$$$ and the first $$$k$$$ for which $$$\min\limits_{i = 1}^{n - k} a_{k + i} - b_i \ge m$$$ using another binary search, and that way, check that the answer is at least $$$m$$$.

solutions.title: Solution (Ne0n25)
solutions.content:
fun main
()
=
repeat
(
readLine
()!!.
toInt
())
{
val n
=
readLine
()!!.
toInt
()
val a
=
readLine
()!!.
split
(
" "
).
map
{
it
.
toInt
()
}
val b
=
readLine
()!!.
split
(
" "
).
map
{
it
.
toInt
()
}
var
ans
=
-
1
var
ans_shift
=
-
1
for
(
shift
in
0
until
n
)
{
var
res
=
1e9
.
toInt
()
for
(
i
in
0
until
n
)
res
=
Math
.
min
(
res
,
Math
.
abs
(
a
[
i
]
-
b
[(
i
+
shift
)
%
n
]))
if
(
res
>
ans
)
{
ans
=
res
			ans_shift
=
shift
}
}
println
(
IntArray
(
n
)
{
(
it
+
ans_shift
)
%
n
+
1
}.
joinToString
(
" "
))
}
